metadata.title:
CONTRACTORS MUST WORK


THEIR MEN EIGHT HOURS

pages.raw_text:
Contractors who have taken contracts for city work
and have failed to obey the provisions of the eghhour
law by working their but eight hours have been not
fed to appear before the board of public works next Mon
day to show why their contracts shall not be
celed


AT the board sent notices to Peter Costello and
r. A. Esick, who have city contracts under way,
which read as follows:


It is reported to the board of public works that in
the execution of your contracts with the city of Spokane
you employing labor in of eight hours in each
calendar day This is in violation of the provisions of
Chapter A of the laws of Washington for 19O3 which act
is expressly made part of your contract with the city
Section of this act provides that it shall be the duty
of all officers agents authorized to contract for work
to be done behalf of the state of any political sub
division created under its laws declare any contract
cancelled when in the execution of such contract the
tractor violates the provisions of the law by employing
labor for than eight hours in any calendar day.


'You hereby accordingly notified to appear before
the board Monday, October H. to show why the
above described contract should not be cancelled
quired by the state.'


AS expected the board will cancel the contracts
of both contractors named above. The contractors
expected to 60 into court and injunction against
the board after which the matter will be thrcshed out in
the courts
